notas.titulo: Alerta por neblinas en el AMBA: visibilidad reducida y una semana con frío

notas.titulo: Aguinaldo de junio 2026: quiénes lo cobran, cuándo se paga y cuánto te corresponde

notas.titulo: Las universidades vuelven a dar clases públicas con reclamos contra Javier Milei para que aplique la Ley de Financiamiento

notas.titulo: Lula’s Stimulus Machine Is Steamrolling Brazil’s Sky-High Interest Rates
notas.bajada: When President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva’s senior officials convene on Mondays to discuss Brazil’s most pressing government business, one rule is clear: keep quiet if you’re not advocating for stimulus.

notas.titulo: Malaysia Secures Fuel Supply Through July, Anwar Says
notas.bajada: Malaysia reiterated that it has secured fuel supply through July, Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim’s office said, supported by continued access to the Strait of Hormuz.

notas.titulo: Japan Sends Officials to Russia to ‘Facilitate Communication’
notas.bajada: Japan has sent senior officials to Russia to keep communication channels open and protect the assets of Japanese companies operating there, in what Tokyo says is the latest in a series of visits that have taken place since the war in…

notas.titulo: S. Korea to Deploy Locally Built Nuclear Submarine by Late-2030s
notas.bajada: South Korea unveiled a roadmap to develop nuclear-powered submarines and deploy the first vessel by the late 2030s, as President Lee Jae Myung convened a new defense strategy council Tuesday focused on strengthening the nation’s military…

notas.titulo: ECB Should Hike Interest Rates in June, Schnabel Says
notas.bajada: The European Central Bank should raise interest rates next month even if there’s a quick resolution to the conflict in the Middle East, according to Executive Board member Isabel Schnabel.

notas.titulo: Chip Bonus Boom Raises Inflation, Housing Risks for BOK, BE Says
notas.bajada: Samsung Electronics Co.’s bonus deal with workers risks turning South Korea’s AI-driven chip boom into a new source of inflation and housing-market pressure that the Bank of Korea will have to address, according to Bloomberg Economics.

notas.titulo: Bloomberg Opinion: How China Can Avoid Mining Disasters
notas.bajada: China's worst mining disaster since 2009 has killed at least 82 people at a pit in Liushenyu, Shanxi province Bloomberg Opinion columnist David Fickling argues that China is increasing safety risks by pushing coal production too hard in…

notas.titulo: ‘Epstein class’ becomes a populist battle cry in US politics
notas.bajada: Accusations of elite impunity loom over November’s midterm elections

notas.titulo: Real wages start to shrink in developed countries
notas.bajada: Strait of Hormuz crisis pushes price growth ahead of pay increases in US, UK and elsewhere

notas.titulo: Hungary Poised to Hold Key Rate With June Cut Seen in Play
notas.bajada: Hungary’s central bank is expected to keep the key interest rate unchanged at the second-highest level in the European Union before potentially weighing monetary easing next month.

notas.titulo: Iran War Boosts $10 Billion Kazakh Rail Plan Linking EU to China
notas.bajada: Kazakhstan’s national railroad operator is expanding its track and infrastructure to meet growing demand for transporting goods between China and Europe as trade flows shift in response to the Iran war.

notas.titulo: BOJ Deputy Chief Underlines Proper Policy as Key for Bond Yields
notas.bajada: Bank of Japan Deputy Governor Ryozo Himino highlighted the importance of appropriate policy adjustments to keep the faith of market participants amid a recent selloff in government debt.

notas.titulo: Charlas de quincho: el baterista enojado, las pantallas del Chiqui Tapia, embutidos radicales y el teatro de Mauricio Macri
notas.bajada: Las palabras de García Cuerva fueron al mentón de La Libertad Avanza, enfrascado en la titánica batalla de hacerles creer a los que la están pasando mal de que en realidad la están pasando bien. Berty se puso al frente, ante un silencio…

notas.titulo: Sri Lanka Surprises With Big Rate Hike as EMs Defend Currencies
notas.bajada: Sri Lanka is the latest emerging market to tighten monetary policy aggressively as an oil crisis triggered by the Iran war pushes currencies to record lows and threatens economic stability.

notas.titulo: Japan’s Katayama Says Will Use Reserve Funds for Subsidies
notas.bajada: The government will use about $3.2 billion from the reserve fund for this year’s annual budget to finance utility subsidies, Japanese Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama said.

notas.titulo: India’s Scorching Nights Strain Power Grid and Threaten Health
notas.bajada: India’s dangerously torrid summer days have long been a climate warning. Now the country is increasingly dealing with sweltering nights too, as global warming and poor urban planning put millions at risk.

notas.titulo: Elecciones: sondeo nacional revela un "empate técnico" entre Javier Milei y Axel Kicillof
notas.bajada: Un informe de CEOP muestra paridad en intención de voto entre ambos dirigentes y confirma una fuerte polarización ideológica en el escenario político actual.

notas.titulo: BYD sube los precios de sus modelos e inicia la preventa del Song Plus (acá se llamará Seal U)
notas.bajada: La irrupción de la marca china BYD en el mercado argentino es una de las noticias más importantes del sector en los últimos meses. Se trata de una de las automotrices más grandes de ese país que está llevando adelante un plan de expansión…

notas.titulo: Japan Slips to World’s No. 3 Creditor Behind Germany, China
notas.bajada: Japan slipped behind China to become the world’s third largest creditor nation in 2025, despite setting a fresh record for its tally of overseas assets.

notas.titulo: Mientras Myriam Bregman crece en Argentina, AOC ya se mueve como candidata presidencial y sacude al Partido Demócrata
notas.bajada: Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez acelera movimientos que ya parecen presidenciales y sacude al Partido Demócrata rumbo a la carrera por la Casa Blanca en 2028.

notas.titulo: Pesadilla en la Justicia: miles de personas ya demandan "como abogados"
notas.bajada: La inteligencia artificial está provocando una explosión de demandas judiciales en EE.UU. Jueces alertan por escritos creados con ChatGPT y Claude.

notas.titulo: Golpe al corazón del negocio de McKinsey
notas.bajada: La revolución de la inteligencia artificial está cambiando algo mucho más profundo que el empleo: también está destruyendo uno de los modelos de negocios más rentables del capitalismo moderno
